text:
A whale stunned viewers by making a surprise appearance during the Olympic surfing semi finals.

Brazil’s Tatiana Weston-Webb and Costa Rica’s Brisa Hennessy were competing for a place in the women’s shortboard surfing final in Teahupo’o, Tahiti on Monday when a humpback whale gatecrashed the event.

As Weston-Webb and Hennessy were waiting for another wave, the whale gave spectators a special moment as it breached in the distance.

Humpback whales, which can grow up 19 metres in length and weigh around 40 tonnes, can be seen in the warm waters surrounding Tahiti during their migration between July and November.

The Olympic surfing events are being hosted in Tahiti – the largest island in French Polynesia – while the majority of events are held nearly 10,000 miles away in Paris.

Weston-Webb beat Hennessy to reach the final but she then lost to the United States’ Caroline Marks, who took the gold medal on Tuesday.

The whale’s appearance in the surfing event has provided the Olympics with another memorable momentafter Brazil’s Gabriel Medina delivered the ‘photo of the Olympics’ last week.

this whale that showed up during#Olympic2024surfing just won the tournament for mepic.twitter.com/4XQVCzW2zI

After scoring a near-perfect 9.90 during his heat against Japan’s Kanoa Igarashi, Medina sprung off his board with his finger in the air.

French photographer, Jerome Brouillet, was on hand to capture the moment and produce one of the iconic images of the Games.

‘The conditions were perfect, the waves were taller than we expected,’ Brouillet said.

‘So he [Medina] is at the back of the wave and I can’t see him and then he pops up and I took four pictures and one of them was this one.

‘It was not hard to take the picture. It was more about anticipating the moment and where Gabriel will kick off the wave.’

title: Whale joins Olympic surfers during semi final in Tahiti | Metro News
description: A whale stunned viewers by making a surprise appearance during the Olympic surfing semi finals.
